Versioning

Source: vignettes/articles/versioning.Rmd
versioning.Rmd
library(peridefs)

Each spec in peridefs tracks algorithm changes over time through a version system. This article explains how versions work, how to request a specific version, and how versioning interacts with the condition/outcome distinction.

Version keys

Versions are stored as named list entries with keys "v1", "v2", "v3", etc. The special string "latest" resolves to the highest-numbered version and is the default for all retrieval functions.

You can inspect which versions a spec has by printing it:

spec_htn
#> 
#> ── Hypertension ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> Condition ID: `htn`
#> 
#> ── Version 1 ──
#> 
#> Condition def: Any of the following: (1) ≥1 inpatient claim with an ICD-9
#> discharge diagnosis code of 401.x, 403.0x, 403.1x, or 403.9x, or an ICD-10
#> discharge diagnosis code of I10, I12.0, I12.9, or I16.x in any discharge
#> diagnosis position; (2) ≥2 physician evaluation and management visit claims
#> with an ICD-9 diagnosis code of 401.x, 403.0x, 403.1x, or 403.9x, or an ICD-10
#> diagnosis code of I10, I12.0, I12.9, or I16.x in any position, at least 30 days
#> apart.
#> Code sets:
#>   `dx_icd9`: 13 condition / 0 outcome codes (401.x, 403.0x, 403.1x, 403.9x)
#>   `dx_icd10`: 7 condition / 0 outcome codes (I10, I12.0, I12.9, I16.x)
#> 
#> ── Version 2 ──
#> 
#> Condition def: Any of the following: (1) ≥1 inpatient claim with an ICD-9
#> discharge diagnosis code of 401.x, 403.0x, 403.1x, or 403.9x, or an ICD-10
#> discharge diagnosis code of I10, I12.0, I12.9, or I16.x in any discharge
#> diagnosis position; (2) ≥2 physician evaluation and management visit claims
#> with an ICD-9 diagnosis code of 401.x, 403.0x, 403.1x, or 403.9x, or an ICD-10
#> diagnosis code of I10, I12.0, I12.9, or I16.x in any position, at least 30 days
#> apart. (3) ≥2 pharmacy fills for an antihypertensive medication (see
#> spec_antihypertensive).
#> Code sets:
#>   `dx_icd9`: 13 condition / 0 outcome codes (401.x, 403.0x, 403.1x, 403.9x)
#>   `dx_icd10`: 7 condition / 0 outcome codes (I10, I12.0, I12.9, I16.x)

Requesting a specific version

Pass the version key or its integer index to any get_* function:

get_htn_codes(version = "v1", code_type = "dx_icd10")
#> $dx_icd10
#> [1] "I10"  "I120" "I129" "I16"  "I160" "I161" "I169"
get_htn_codes(version = "latest", code_type = "dx_icd10")
#> $dx_icd10
#> [1] "I10"  "I120" "I129" "I16"  "I160" "I161" "I169"

Integer shortcuts also work — version = 1 is equivalent to version = "v1":

get_htn_codes(version = 1, code_type = "dx_icd10")
#> $dx_icd10
#> [1] "I10"  "I120" "I129" "I16"  "I160" "I161" "I169"

What changes between versions?

Three things can differ across versions:

  1. Codes — the set of ICD/HCPCS codes changes (codes added, removed, or reclassified).
  2. Narrative definitions — the algorithm description is updated without changing the codes.
  3. Code membership flags — a code that was previously in both the condition and outcome definitions may be restricted to one.

When codes are identical across versions (only the narrative changed), the spec stores them once and applies the same code set to both versions.

Condition vs. outcome definitions

Many condition specs carry two parallel definitions:

  • "condition" — used to identify a comorbidity or history in a lookback window (e.g., “patient had hypertension in the 12 months before index”).
  • "outcome" — used to identify a new event during follow-up (e.g., “patient had a new AMI after index”).

Use the variable_type argument to select which definition to retrieve:

get_ascvd_codes(variable_type = "condition", code_type = "dx_icd10") |> head()
#> $dx_icd10
#>   [1] "I21"    "I210"   "I2101"  "I2102"  "I2109"  "I211"   "I2111"  "I2119" 
#>   [9] "I212"   "I2121"  "I2129"  "I213"   "I214"   "I219"   "I21A"   "I21A1" 
#>  [17] "I21A9"  "I22"    "I220"   "I221"   "I222"   "I228"   "I229"   "I2510" 
#>  [25] "I25810" "I25811" "I25812" "I253"   "I2541"  "I2542"  "Z951"   "Z9861" 
#>  [33] "I200"   "I201"   "I208"   "I209"   "I240"   "I241"   "I248"   "I252"  
#>  [41] "I255"   "I2582"  "I2583"  "I2584"  "I2589"  "I259"   "I63"    "I630"  
#>  [49] "I6300"  "I6301"  "I63011" "I63012" "I63013" "I63019" "I6302"  "I6303" 
#>  [57] "I63031" "I63032" "I63033" "I63039" "I6309"  "I631"   "I6310"  "I6311" 
#>  [65] "I63111" "I63112" "I63113" "I63119" "I6312"  "I6313"  "I63131" "I63132"
#>  [73] "I63133" "I63139" "I6319"  "I632"   "I6320"  "I6321"  "I63211" "I63212"
#>  [81] "I63213" "I63219" "I6322"  "I6323"  "I63231" "I63232" "I63233" "I63239"
#>  [89] "I6329"  "I633"   "I6330"  "I6331"  "I63311" "I63312" "I63313" "I63319"
#>  [97] "I6332"  "I63321" "I63322" "I63323" "I63329" "I6333"  "I63331" "I63332"
#> [105] "I63333" "I63339" "I6334"  "I63341" "I63342" "I63343" "I63349" "I6339" 
#> [113] "I634"   "I6340"  "I6341"  "I63411" "I63412" "I63413" "I63419" "I6342" 
#> [121] "I63421" "I63422" "I63423" "I63429" "I6343"  "I63431" "I63432" "I63433"
#> [129] "I63439" "I6344"  "I63441" "I63442" "I63443" "I63449" "I6349"  "I635"  
#> [137] "I6350"  "I6351"  "I63511" "I63512" "I63513" "I63519" "I6352"  "I63521"
#> [145] "I63522" "I63523" "I63529" "I6353"  "I63531" "I63532" "I63533" "I63539"
#> [153] "I6354"  "I63541" "I63542" "I63543" "I63549" "I6359"  "I636"   "I638"  
#> [161] "I6381"  "I6389"  "I639"   "I702"   "I7020"  "I70201" "I70202" "I70203"
#> [169] "I70208" "I70209" "I7021"  "I70211" "I70212" "I70213" "I70218" "I70219"
#> [177] "I7022"  "I70221" "I70222" "I70223" "I70228" "I70229" "I7023"  "I70231"
#> [185] "I70232" "I70233" "I70234" "I70235" "I70238" "I70239" "I7024"  "I70241"
#> [193] "I70242" "I70243" "I70244" "I70245" "I70248" "I70249" "I7025"  "I7026" 
#> [201] "I70261" "I70262" "I70263" "I70268" "I70269" "I7029"  "I70291" "I70292"
#> [209] "I70293" "I70298" "I70299" "I703"   "I7030"  "I70301" "I70302" "I70303"
#> [217] "I70308" "I70309" "I7031"  "I70311" "I70312" "I70313" "I70318" "I70319"
#> [225] "I7032"  "I70321" "I70322" "I70323" "I70328" "I70329" "I7033"  "I70331"
#> [233] "I70332" "I70333" "I70334" "I70335" "I70338" "I70339" "I7034"  "I70341"
#> [241] "I70342" "I70343" "I70344" "I70345" "I70348" "I70349" "I7035"  "I7036" 
#> [249] "I70361" "I70362" "I70363" "I70368" "I70369" "I7039"  "I70391" "I70392"
#> [257] "I70393" "I70398" "I70399" "I704"   "I7040"  "I70401" "I70402" "I70403"
#> [265] "I70408" "I70409" "I7041"  "I70411" "I70412" "I70413" "I70418" "I70419"
#> [273] "I7042"  "I70421" "I70422" "I70423" "I70428" "I70429" "I7043"  "I70431"
#> [281] "I70432" "I70433" "I70434" "I70435" "I70438" "I70439" "I7044"  "I70441"
#> [289] "I70442" "I70443" "I70444" "I70445" "I70448" "I70449" "I7045"  "I7046" 
#> [297] "I70461" "I70462" "I70463" "I70468" "I70469" "I7049"  "I70491" "I70492"
#> [305] "I70493" "I70498" "I70499" "I705"   "I7050"  "I70501" "I70502" "I70503"
#> [313] "I70508" "I70509" "I7051"  "I70511" "I70512" "I70513" "I70518" "I70519"
#> [321] "I7052"  "I70521" "I70522" "I70523" "I70528" "I70529" "I7053"  "I70531"
#> [329] "I70532" "I70533" "I70534" "I70535" "I70538" "I70539" "I7054"  "I70541"
#> [337] "I70542" "I70543" "I70544" "I70545" "I70548" "I70549" "I7055"  "I7056" 
#> [345] "I70561" "I70562" "I70563" "I70568" "I70569" "I7059"  "I70591" "I70592"
#> [353] "I70593" "I70598" "I70599" "I706"   "I7060"  "I70601" "I70602" "I70603"
#> [361] "I70608" "I70609" "I7061"  "I70611" "I70612" "I70613" "I70618" "I70619"
#> [369] "I7062"  "I70621" "I70622" "I70623" "I70628" "I70629" "I7063"  "I70631"
#> [377] "I70632" "I70633" "I70634" "I70635" "I70638" "I70639" "I7064"  "I70641"
#> [385] "I70642" "I70643" "I70644" "I70645" "I70648" "I70649" "I7065"  "I7066" 
#> [393] "I70661" "I70662" "I70663" "I70668" "I70669" "I7069"  "I70691" "I70692"
#> [401] "I70693" "I70698" "I70699" "I707"   "I7070"  "I70701" "I70702" "I70703"
#> [409] "I70708" "I70709" "I7071"  "I70711" "I70712" "I70713" "I70718" "I70719"
#> [417] "I7072"  "I70721" "I70722" "I70723" "I70728" "I70729" "I7073"  "I70731"
#> [425] "I70732" "I70733" "I70734" "I70735" "I70738" "I70739" "I7074"  "I70741"
#> [433] "I70742" "I70743" "I70744" "I70745" "I70748" "I70749" "I7075"  "I7076" 
#> [441] "I70761" "I70762" "I70763" "I70768" "I70769" "I7079"  "I70791" "I70792"
#> [449] "I70793" "I70798" "I70799" "I709"   "I7092"  "I739"
get_ascvd_codes(variable_type = "outcome",   code_type = "dx_icd10") |> head()
#> $dx_icd10
#>   [1] "I21"    "I210"   "I2101"  "I2102"  "I2109"  "I211"   "I2111"  "I2119" 
#>   [9] "I212"   "I2121"  "I2129"  "I213"   "I214"   "I219"   "I21A"   "I21A1" 
#>  [17] "I21A9"  "I22"    "I220"   "I221"   "I222"   "I228"   "I229"   "I60"   
#>  [25] "I600"   "I6000"  "I6001"  "I6002"  "I601"   "I6010"  "I6011"  "I6012" 
#>  [33] "I602"   "I603"   "I6030"  "I6031"  "I6032"  "I604"   "I605"   "I6050" 
#>  [41] "I6051"  "I6052"  "I606"   "I607"   "I608"   "I609"   "I61"    "I610"  
#>  [49] "I611"   "I612"   "I613"   "I614"   "I615"   "I616"   "I618"   "I619"  
#>  [57] "I63"    "I630"   "I6300"  "I6301"  "I63011" "I63012" "I63013" "I63019"
#>  [65] "I6302"  "I6303"  "I63031" "I63032" "I63033" "I63039" "I6309"  "I631"  
#>  [73] "I6310"  "I6311"  "I63111" "I63112" "I63113" "I63119" "I6312"  "I6313" 
#>  [81] "I63131" "I63132" "I63133" "I63139" "I6319"  "I632"   "I6320"  "I6321" 
#>  [89] "I63211" "I63212" "I63213" "I63219" "I6322"  "I6323"  "I63231" "I63232"
#>  [97] "I63233" "I63239" "I6329"  "I633"   "I6330"  "I6331"  "I63311" "I63312"
#> [105] "I63313" "I63319" "I6332"  "I63321" "I63322" "I63323" "I63329" "I6333" 
#> [113] "I63331" "I63332" "I63333" "I63339" "I6334"  "I63341" "I63342" "I63343"
#> [121] "I63349" "I6339"  "I634"   "I6340"  "I6341"  "I63411" "I63412" "I63413"
#> [129] "I63419" "I6342"  "I63421" "I63422" "I63423" "I63429" "I6343"  "I63431"
#> [137] "I63432" "I63433" "I63439" "I6344"  "I63441" "I63442" "I63443" "I63449"
#> [145] "I6349"  "I635"   "I6350"  "I6351"  "I63511" "I63512" "I63513" "I63519"
#> [153] "I6352"  "I63521" "I63522" "I63523" "I63529" "I6353"  "I63531" "I63532"
#> [161] "I63533" "I63539" "I6354"  "I63541" "I63542" "I63543" "I63549" "I6359" 
#> [169] "I636"   "I638"   "I6381"  "I6389"  "I639"   "I6789"  "I67850" "I67858"
#> [177] "I702"   "I7020"  "I70201" "I70202" "I70203" "I70208" "I70209" "I7021" 
#> [185] "I70211" "I70212" "I70213" "I70218" "I70219" "I7022"  "I70221" "I70222"
#> [193] "I70223" "I70228" "I70229" "I7023"  "I70231" "I70232" "I70233" "I70234"
#> [201] "I70235" "I70238" "I70239" "I7024"  "I70241" "I70242" "I70243" "I70244"
#> [209] "I70245" "I70248" "I70249" "I7025"  "I7026"  "I70261" "I70262" "I70263"
#> [217] "I70268" "I70269" "I7029"  "I70291" "I70292" "I70293" "I70298" "I70299"
#> [225] "I703"   "I7030"  "I70301" "I70302" "I70303" "I70308" "I70309" "I7031" 
#> [233] "I70311" "I70312" "I70313" "I70318" "I70319" "I7032"  "I70321" "I70322"
#> [241] "I70323" "I70328" "I70329" "I7033"  "I70331" "I70332" "I70333" "I70334"
#> [249] "I70335" "I70338" "I70339" "I7034"  "I70341" "I70342" "I70343" "I70344"
#> [257] "I70345" "I70348" "I70349" "I7035"  "I7036"  "I70361" "I70362" "I70363"
#> [265] "I70368" "I70369" "I7039"  "I70391" "I70392" "I70393" "I70398" "I70399"
#> [273] "I704"   "I7040"  "I70401" "I70402" "I70403" "I70408" "I70409" "I7041" 
#> [281] "I70411" "I70412" "I70413" "I70418" "I70419" "I7042"  "I70421" "I70422"
#> [289] "I70423" "I70428" "I70429" "I7043"  "I70431" "I70432" "I70433" "I70434"
#> [297] "I70435" "I70438" "I70439" "I7044"  "I70441" "I70442" "I70443" "I70444"
#> [305] "I70445" "I70448" "I70449" "I7045"  "I7046"  "I70461" "I70462" "I70463"
#> [313] "I70468" "I70469" "I7049"  "I70491" "I70492" "I70493" "I70498" "I70499"
#> [321] "I705"   "I7050"  "I70501" "I70502" "I70503" "I70508" "I70509" "I7051" 
#> [329] "I70511" "I70512" "I70513" "I70518" "I70519" "I7052"  "I70521" "I70522"
#> [337] "I70523" "I70528" "I70529" "I7053"  "I70531" "I70532" "I70533" "I70534"
#> [345] "I70535" "I70538" "I70539" "I7054"  "I70541" "I70542" "I70543" "I70544"
#> [353] "I70545" "I70548" "I70549" "I7055"  "I7056"  "I70561" "I70562" "I70563"
#> [361] "I70568" "I70569" "I7059"  "I70591" "I70592" "I70593" "I70598" "I70599"
#> [369] "I706"   "I7060"  "I70601" "I70602" "I70603" "I70608" "I70609" "I7061" 
#> [377] "I70611" "I70612" "I70613" "I70618" "I70619" "I7062"  "I70621" "I70622"
#> [385] "I70623" "I70628" "I70629" "I7063"  "I70631" "I70632" "I70633" "I70634"
#> [393] "I70635" "I70638" "I70639" "I7064"  "I70641" "I70642" "I70643" "I70644"
#> [401] "I70645" "I70648" "I70649" "I7065"  "I7066"  "I70661" "I70662" "I70663"
#> [409] "I70668" "I70669" "I7069"  "I70691" "I70692" "I70693" "I70698" "I70699"
#> [417] "I707"   "I7070"  "I70701" "I70702" "I70703" "I70708" "I70709" "I7071" 
#> [425] "I70711" "I70712" "I70713" "I70718" "I70719" "I7072"  "I70721" "I70722"
#> [433] "I70723" "I70728" "I70729" "I7073"  "I70731" "I70732" "I70733" "I70734"
#> [441] "I70735" "I70738" "I70739" "I7074"  "I70741" "I70742" "I70743" "I70744"
#> [449] "I70745" "I70748" "I70749" "I7075"  "I7076"  "I70761" "I70762" "I70763"
#> [457] "I70768" "I70769" "I7079"  "I70791" "I70792" "I70793" "I70798" "I70799"
#> [465] "I709"   "I7092"  "I739"

The two sets can overlap, be identical, or be completely distinct depending on the condition. Narrative definitions also differ:

get_ascvd_defs(variable_type = "condition")
#> [1] "History of coronary heart disease (CHD) or cerebrovascular disease. Note: lower extremity artery disease (LEAD/PAD) is also part of this definition but its spec is not yet implemented. See spec_chd (v1 condition) and spec_cvd (v1 condition)."
get_ascvd_defs(variable_type = "outcome")
#> [1] "Event of coronary heart disease (CHD) or ischemic or any stroke. Note: lower extremity artery disease (LEAD/PAD) is also part of this definition but its spec is not yet implemented. See spec_chd (v1 outcome) and spec_stroke (v1 outcome)."

Adding a new version to a custom spec

Use modify_code_spec() with new_version = TRUE to append a version to any spec (bundled or custom-built). See vignette("custom-specs") for examples.

On this page

Developed by Perisphere RWE — health outcomes, epidemiology and biostatistics consulting.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
